 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33mv","title":"demo_showcase UX: define product concept + information architecture","description":"Pick a single cohesive "product" story and define the information architecture.

Recommendation (unless a better idea emerges): **Charmed Control Center** — an ops-style console for a fictional platform (services, deployments, jobs, logs, docs, settings).

Define:
- Primary navigation items (pages) and what each proves about the stack.
- Layout primitives (app chrome): header (status), sidebar (nav), main content, footer (hints).
- Core user journeys:
  - Navigate dashboards, drill into a service/job, view logs, open docs, run a wizard, browse files.

Acceptance:
- Clear page list with 1-2 sentences each describing: goal, components used, and what makes it "real".","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.263653172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678896124Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678828398Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive product concept doc at docs/demo_showcase/PRODUCT_CONCEPT.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33mv","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3449","title":"E2E scenario: wizard -> job -> logs correlation","description":"Scenario: complete the wizard to start a deployment/job, then verify it shows up elsewhere.

Steps:
- Open Wizard
- Fill inputs (including paste for one field)
- Confirm
- Observe job created in Jobs page
- Observe correlated log entries in Logs page

Assertions:
- Domain state changes are consistent across pages.
- Logs include fields referencing the created job/deployment.

Acceptance:
- Proves cross-page integration and the domain action API.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.600509120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850246365Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850177547Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-1x3q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-2qq7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-34xw","title":"Conformance unit tests must fail if any fixture is skipped","description":"Currently the per-crate conformance unit tests treat "SKIPPED:" fixtures as neither pass nor fail and only assert failed==0. This can hide missing fixture implementations and violates the project's 100% feature-for-feature parity requirement.

Fix: In each conformance test (bubbles, bubbletea, lipgloss, harmonica, glamour, glow, huh, charmed_log) assert skipped==0 (and/or treat SKIPPED as failure) so CI cannot go green with incomplete coverage.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:39:06.529499533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:29:09.034055497Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:29:09.033990767Z","close_reason":"Completed: conformance runners hard-fail on any SKIPPED fixtures","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","tests"]}
 {"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

Integration:
- Must plug into the structured artifact logger (`E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture`).

Acceptance:
- Scenarios are readable scripts: "press j", "open docs", "search", "quit".
- Runner failures explain *where* and *why* (step id + last frame + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.886754241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.460032517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.459867219Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-366a","title":"Integration Tests: glamour + lipgloss styling","description":"Integration tests for glamour markdown with lipgloss styles.

## Purpose
Verify glamour correctly uses lipgloss for styled output
and that styles compose without corruption.

## Test Requirements

### Style Application
- [ ] glamour Style maps to lipgloss Styles
- [ ] Custom themes apply correctly
- [ ] Default themes render expected output

### Rendering Integration
- [ ] Markdown elements get correct styles
- [ ] Code blocks get syntax highlighting colors
- [ ] Nested styles (bold in italic) work
- [ ] Links have distinct styling

### Width Handling
- [ ] glamour respects lipgloss width
- [ ] Word wrap accounts for ANSI codes
- [ ] Padding/margin calculations correct

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Very wide content with styles
- [ ] Many nested style changes
- [ ] Styles across line breaks

## Success Criteria
- Styled markdown renders identically to Go charm
- No style leakage between elements
- Width calculations match expected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:49.408003481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534265045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534133109Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","integration","lipgloss","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-15c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
